finding_remediation.finding: bd-2yhs promised a CI workflow, detector, and findings report but prior evidence did not cite committed code artifacts or committed CI proof.
finding_remediation.resolution: The duplicate-implementation detector is now wired into a committed GitHub Actions workflow and this evidence bundle cites the detector, section gate, E2E suite, policy registry, and report artifacts.

code_artifacts.purpose: Runs the duplicate-implementation detector, section 10.N gate, and semantic-boundary E2E suite on relevant source, policy, script, and workflow changes.
Detects cross-track capability redefinitions, protected semantic-family drift, and forbidden internal boundary imports.
Aggregates the 10.N ownership registry, duplicate-implementation gate, oracle close-condition gate, and cross-track linter checks.
Covers rule catalog shape, waiver handling, ownership matching, semantic-boundary drift detection, contract alignment, and forbidden internal imports.
Exercises pass and fail CI scenarios with fixture workspaces and stable rule IDs.
Machine-readable canonical ownership map consumed by the detector.
Human-readable semantic-boundary contract kept aligned with detector rule IDs and sanctioned path sets.
Scoped, auditable waiver input for duplicate-implementation gate exceptions.

local_validation.observed: ownership-check/v2 verdict PASS; 393 Rust files scanned; 0 active violations
